contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 45
- Description: Wire packaging photos into AI warm-up pipeline as default attachments
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/go_to_market/pull/74
  
  Details:
  ## What changed
  
  Made on-shelf packaging photos a **default attachment** on every initial warm-up email generated by the AI pipeline (`scripts/suggest_warmup_prospect_drafts.py` in `go_to_market`). First-touch warm-ups now ship with three attachments: wholesale price list PDF + bag-front photo + bag-back photo.
  
  - `retail_price_list/agroverse_packaging_front.jpeg` — new asset (768x1024, 72 KB), bag front.
  - `retail_price_list/agroverse_packaging_back.jpeg` — new asset (1125x1500, 334 KB), bag back. Original source was 3024x4032 / 1.7 MB; resized before checkin to keep the email payload reasonable and avoid bloating the repo with a 1.7 MB binary.
  - `scripts/suggest_warmup_prospect_drafts.py`:
    - Added `_DEFAULT_PACKAGING_FRONT` / `_DEFAULT_PACKAGING_BACK` constants.
    - Renamed `build_message_raw_with_pdf` to `build_message_raw_with_attachments` and added an `image_paths` kwarg so the helper can append additional image attachments after the PDF.
    - Added CLI flags `--packaging-front` / `--packaging-back` (empty string to skip).
    - Updated body template, Grok system prompt, and user-context line to reference all three attachments.
    - Updated `Email Agent Drafts.notes` column to log all attached filenames.
  
  Locally verified MIME structure for both the plain-only path and the `--track-opens` / `--track-clicks` HTML-alternative path; total RFC822 size around 744 KB, well below Gmail's 25 MB ceiling.
  
  ## Why
  
  Retailers (Berkeley Herbal Center after the wrong-address redirect, Jupiter Row, Go Ask Alice) have repeatedly asked to see how the bag actually looks on shelf before deciding to stock. Until now warm-ups carried only the wholesale list, so this gap kept showing up in feedback. Making the photos a default attachment closes the gap consistently for every future first-touch warm-up.
  
  ## Pull requests
  
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/go_to_market/pull/74 (merged)
- Contributor(s): Garyjob <garyjob@gmail.com>
- TDG Issued: 75.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
